behaviors.description: Out-of-stock products are excluded even if they have high relevance scores.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because the failure boundary is filter-stage precedence over ranking-stage output (module handoff: "regardless of their relevance score"). Integration beats System because behavior text does not require real HTTP/OS/subprocess, only in-process pipeline wiring. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML model judgment in behavior text, and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real filter + ranking modules with an in-memory catalog containing high-score out-of-stock items; assert final returned list contains no out-of-stock products.

behaviors.description: Blocked user categories are excluded from recommendations.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because bug appears at settings-to-filter handoff (blocked categories from user settings must propagate correctly). Integration beats System because no real OS/network/subprocess is required by the behavior text. Rejected higher levels: behavior is deterministic rule application, not AI/ML output quality or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Use real settings retrieval module (backed by local in-memory repo) + category filter; set blocked categories and verify excluded categories never appear in response.

behaviors.description: Products with recent browsing signal rank above products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because this behavior is core ranking logic within one algorithm boundary (signal weighting), not required module interaction. Unit beats System because behavior text has no real infra dependency. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML call is specified; ranking criterion is deterministic feature weighting.
behaviors.test_description: Call ranking/scoring function with controlled product features where only browsing signal differs; assert browsing-matching products receive higher scores/order.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page with stable ordering across repeated requests when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because stability requirement is across repeated service calls where ranking output and pagination boundaries interact. Integration beats System because stability can be validated with in-process modules and fixed data snapshot; behavior text does not require real network/OS effects. Rejected higher levels: no model judgment quality or multi-agent chaining described.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end recommendation assembly (rank + sort + paginate) twice against unchanged in-memory state; request page 2 both times and assert identical item IDs and order.

behaviors.description: At least one recommendation comes from an unseen category (serendipity).
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Unit beats Integration because this is a local reranking/diversity rule in the algorithm, testable in one function boundary. Unit beats System because no real infra is needed by behavior text. Rejected higher levels: requirement is deterministic rule satisfaction, not AI/ML semantic relevance judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke diversity/serendipity selection logic with candidate list dominated by seen categories and verify output includes at least one unseen-category product.

behaviors.description: Cold-start users get popularity-based recommendations instead of empty results.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because failure is often in orchestration between history lookup (empty) and fallback strategy (popularity source). Integration beats System because local module wiring with in-memory/local DB is sufficient; no real OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependence or agentic chain is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Execute recommendation service for a user with no history using real fallback path to popularity provider module; assert non-empty results and that ranking source is popularity-based.

behaviors.description: Click tracking stores correct product ID, rank position, and timestamp for each clicked recommendation.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because correctness depends on data handoff between recommendation list context and tracking persistence fields. Integration beats System because local persistence and controllable clock can reproduce real mapping bugs without real network/OS primitives. Rejected higher levels: behavior text specifies deterministic event logging fields, not AI/ML judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Serve a recommendation list, simulate click on a given position, persist event via real tracking module + local DB, and assert stored product_id/position/timestamp match expected values.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because this is end-to-end performance across multiple components, not single-function correctness. System beats Integration because latency SLO failure depends on real runtime characteristics (process scheduling, memory behavior, real datastore/index access) that mocks/in-process fakes can hide. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML model call or multi-agent sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real recommendation service process against a realistic 100k-product dataset and measure end-to-end request latency under controlled load; fail if p95 (or defined metric) exceeds 200ms.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real process/runtime benchmarking; lower-level tests cannot reliably catch true SLO regressions.

behaviors.description: After purchase, that product is deprioritized in later recommendations.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because behavior spans event ingestion/state update and subsequent ranking request (cross-module state handoff). Integration beats System because this can be reproduced with local persistence and in-process modules; no required real HTTP/OS/subprocess in behavior text. Rejected higher levels: deprioritization rule is deterministic and not based on AI/ML output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Generate initial recommendations, record purchase event for one recommended product, regenerate recommendations, and assert purchased product rank drops relative to baseline.

behaviors.description: With unchanged user state, repeated recommendation requests return identical ranked lists.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise: Integration beats Unit because determinism is validated across repeated full pipeline invocations, where ordering/pagination/state assembly interactions can introduce drift. Integration beats System because behavior text does not require real external infrastructure; in-process wiring with fixed state is sufficient. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML nondeterminism is specified and no multi-step agent chain is described.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke recommendation endpoint/service twice for the same frozen user/profile/catalog state and assert identical ordered product IDs across responses.
